items.title: Police charge 13yo girl after alleged crime rampage in Melbourne
items.summary: Victoria Police say the teenager was driving a stolen car when she swerved towards a group of people from the Jewish community on Saturday.

items.title: WA's greyhound racing regulator defends track record at inquiry into sport's future
items.summary: The body governing greyhound racing in WA says it is determined to maintain the welfare of both the dogs and the industry at a parliamentary inquiry, which was sparked by a petition that garnered 26,000 signatures.

items.title: Live: Socceroos smash Curaçao 5-1 in World Cup farewell
items.summary: The Socceroos have racked up quick second half goals to beat Curaçao 5-1 in their World Cup farewell match in Melbourne.

items.title: Zac Lomax available to make Super Rugby Pacific debut if selected for Western Force
items.summary: Zac Lomax could get his first taste of rugby union at a venue where the Western Force recruit is no stranger to receiving a hostile welcome.

items.title: Australian Antarctic Division's cultural workplace reforms yield mixed results
items.summary: Workplace reforms implemented by the Australian Antarctic Division (AAD) after two damning reports into the organisation's culture have led to some improvements but a review has found problems persist.

items.title: Why these women have never had a partner
items.summary: Three women share their experiences of never having a partner — not by choice — and what that's like when you wish you did.

items.title: 'Welcome to Country' pioneer farewelled on her beloved Bundjalung Country
items.summary: Rhoda Roberts AO is being remembered for her ability to laugh and embrace life, as well as her contribution to the Australian arts and Indigenous cultural movements.

items.title: Liam Alexander Hall may enter insanity plea over alleged terrorism act at Perth Invasion Day rally
items.summary: A man accused of tossing a homemade "fragment bomb" filled with ball bearings and screws into a crowd in Perth's CBD flags through his lawyer that he may argue he is not criminally responsible for his actions due to insanity.

items.title: Salary dramas, blown draft picks and scandals — How Essendon and Carlton found themselves in AFL purgatory
items.summary: Essendon and Carlton were once mainstays when it came to the business end of the AFL season. Over the last quarter century, both clubs have fallen on hard times. Here's why.

items.title: Dezi Freeman hid at a property perfectly designed for someone wanting to live off-grid
items.summary: The rural property where police finally caught up with the fugitive gunman was a good place to disappear.

items.title: How Porepunkah learned the search for Dezi Freeman was over
items.summary: Seven months after a gunman killed two police officers on a nearby property, the tiny town woke to news Dezi Freeman was dead.

items.title: Jackie 'O' Henderson files legal action against ARN Media
items.summary: Former Sydney radio broadcaster Jackie "O" Henderson has filed legal action against employer ARN Media, following the collapse of the hit Kyle and Jackie O Show.

items.title: RBA to remove surcharges on debit, credit cards, card networks including eftpos, Mastercard and Visa
items.summary: The removal of surcharges on debit and credit cards is one of the sweeping reforms from the Reserve Bank.

items.title: Consumer confidence hits 53-year low, ASX gains — as it happened
items.summary: Consumer confidence across Australia has hit its worst low since tracking began in 1973, according to the latest ANZ-Roy Morgan survey, but the ASX 200 has bounced back to finish the last day of the month with modest gains. Catch up on the day's events on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Live: Israel's ground invasion of Lebanon is '100pc defensive', ambassador says
items.summary: Israel's ambassador to Australia says the incursion of ground troops into southern Lebanon is "100 per cent defensive" as Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says the country is "beyond the halfway point" in achieving its Iran war goals. Follow live.

items.title: The 'miracle' drugs giving children with spinal muscular atrophy another shot at life
items.summary: Children born with a rare neuromuscular disorder are being given another chance in life thanks to a new treatment experts are calling a "game-changer".

items.title: Israel passes death penalty for Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks
items.summary: The law, long-sought by Israel's far-right politicians, makes death by hanging the default punishment for West Bank Palestinians convicted of killing Israelis.

items.title: The Matildas hype is real. So why are the A-League Women crowds still so small?
items.summary: Despite the recent hype of the Matildas, support for the A-League Women remains relatively low.

items.title: Italy's World Cup fate to be decided, Socceroos to learn final group opponent
items.summary: The last of the 48 teams for the FIFA World Cup will be decided tomorrow, with Italy hoping to end a 12-year exile, while Türkiye and Kosovo look to join the Socceroos' group.

items.title: 50 per cent of Australians avoid health care they need due to cost, according to Consumers Health Forum report
items.summary: One in two Australians missed out on health care they needed last year mainly because they could not afford it, according to a new report by the peak healthcare consumers group.

items.title: Bittersweet Iraqi film The President's Cake brings hope as war rages in the Middle East
items.summary: Filmmaker Hasan Hadi had to bring flowers to school to celebrate Saddam Hussein’s birthday. That memory loosely informs his beautiful debut feature, The President’s Cake.

items.title: If your home is underinsured it comes with big risks, experts say
items.summary: If your home is underinsured, it means your insurance policy won't fully cover the cost of rebuilding, repairing or replacing it.

items.title: From tax cuts to price gouging crackdowns, how G7 nations are combating the fuel crisis
items.summary: Australia is not alone in weathering rising fuel costs and issues with domestic supply and distribution amid the war in the Middle East. G7 nations are also working out ways to ease the crisis.

items.title: Israel Defense Forces admit to posting fake image of Lebanese journalist killed in strike
items.summary: The IDF has admitted the image of a killed journalist in military uniform it posted on its official X account was faked.

items.title: Government halves fuel excise for three months to ease cost of petrol, diesel
items.summary: The government has halved the fuel excise on petrol and diesel for three months, reducing the cost of fuel by 26.3 cents per litre.

items.title: Contemporary regional life looms large in major Brad Rimmer exhibition
items.summary: Western Australian documentary photographer Brad Rimmer's beautiful, melancholy images of Wheatbelt life sprawl through Fremantle gallery.

items.title: Antarctic penguin appears on remote WA beach, 4,000km from home
items.summary: The king penguin, known for being the second-largest penguin species and living on ice-free parts of the sub-Antarctic, was rescued near Esperance on WA's south coast.

items.title: Dezi Freeman killed by police in Victoria's north-east after seven-month hunt
items.summary: Freeman had been wanted since last August over the fatal shooting of two police officers at Porepunkah.

items.title: Yerrabi Yurwang Health Hub closing due to lack of funding, leaving First Nations patients in Canberra in the lurch
items.summary: An absence of funding from the ACT and federal governments is forcing the Yerrabi Yurwang Health Hub to close this week, affecting about 400 First Nations patients.

items.title: How to approach your employer about working from home and flexible work arrangements
items.summary: With rising fuel prices adding additional financial stress to household living costs, what are your rights when it comes to working from home — and what are the best ways to approach your employer about flexible work arrangements?

items.title: From Australian Idol to The Piano, Guy Sebastian has tried most types of reality TV
items.summary: Singing his way into the Australian public's hearts way back in 2003, Guy Sebastian has since had a fascinating and varied career. We catch up with the star to find out about the little things that keep him going — from his two sons to his favourite TV show.

items.title: When a connection feels like home
items.summary: After cancelling a full Sri Lankan wedding and a string of "disastrous" relationships, Sashi Perera found herself opposite a man who made believing in him simple.

items.title: In the Strait of Hormuz, ships make a bargain to negotiate their safe passage
items.summary: Iran is running one of the world's most critical shipping lanes as a "toll booth" and has likely made hundreds of millions of dollars of extra income from selling its own oil since the US and Israel waged war against the regime.

items.title: End of an era for Tharwa General Store after a century being owned by one family
items.summary: The Tharwa General Store — which has been run by the same family since 1926 — has been many things: a post office, a mechanic's workshop, a fuel stop, a lolly shop, a watering hole, and above all, a place where a community comes together.

items.title: Vanished: Six months of searching for Gus Lamont
items.summary: Six months to the day since four-year-old Gus Lamont was reported missing from a vast outback station, many more questions than answers remain.

items.title: Tiwi Islanders eager to have their say as remote voting for the federal election begins
items.summary: Voters in the small remote community of Pirlangimpi on the Tiwi Islands, north of Darwin, have become some of the first to cast their votes for the federal election.

items.title: Energy projects in South West WA threaten votes for major federal parties
items.summary: Australia's two major parties have tied their federal election fates to controversial energy projects in WA's South West where the issues have polarised voters.

items.title: Cost of living, housing among key concerns in SA marginal seat of Boothby
items.summary: At this year’s federal election, the South Australian seat of Boothby is one of the state’s two marginal seats. As part of the ABC’s “Your Say” campaign, we spoke to residents in the electorate about their biggest concerns.

items.title: Melbourne seat of Calwell highlights Labor woes as Gaza pain galvanises Muslim voters
items.summary: Dissatisfaction with the Albanese government's stance on Israel-Palestine among many Arab and Muslim voters threatens to unseat Labor MPs, in some places for the first time.

items.title: Next-generation voters call for better engagement ahead of federal election
items.summary: Young people in Albury say issues that directly affect them and their communities are not being addressed by major parties for the 2025 election campaign.

items.title: Graduates in 'debt vortex' as HECS front and centre for first time in an election
items.summary: New research shows a sharp rise in HECS debts, despite graduate earning power declining, as student loans become a potent election issue for the first time.

items.title: Australians tell ABC's Your Say they are struggling more than ever with housing costs
items.summary: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they are struggling more than ever with housing costs.

items.title: Update your ABC News homepage for more local stories
items.summary: We've got an ABC News homepage for you. Adjust your settings for the right mix of local and national news.
